steps.text: Determine whether fuel was inspected, handled and maintained as required, since the last inspection.
Determine whether the licensee’s fuel handling procedures are adequate to perform intended functions.
Determine whether fuel is moved and inspected consistent with the requirements of the TS and the licensee’s procedures.
Determine whether fuel handling activities are conducted in accordance with 10 CFR Part 20 and the licensee’s procedures and programs for radiation protection.
Determine whether the licensee satisfied security plan requirements for fuel movement activities.
Determine whether significant fuel movement or inspection problems are identified and resolved in accordance with the licensee’s procedural controls.
Determine whether the licensee was within TS limits and met procedural requirements before resuming normal operation after fuel movement.

constraints.text: Handling of fuel must be in accordance with TS requirements and applicable procedures.
Fuel handling activities must be consistent with procedures that prevent accidental criticality.

steps.text: Inspect fuel and control rods in accordance with the TS and their procedures.
Check the control rods to determine their capability to continue to function.
Investigate and resolve failures of control rods.
Inspect an installed neutron source for leakage paths such as splitting or pitting if it is removed during refueling.
Observe radiological controls during one fuel movement activity.
Review radiation protection records and discuss with responsible personnel for the most recent fuel movement activity.

constraints.text: Maintain occupational doses as low as reasonably achievable (ALARA).

steps.text: Perform grab sampling for airborne radioactive particulate matter in accordance with approved licensee procedures.
Monitor exits from controlled areas using a Geiger Muller (GM) detector in accordance with 10 CFR Part 20 and licensee's procedural requirements.
Perform surveys for contamination in accordance with licensee procedures.
Monitor radiation areas in accordance with Subpart F of 10 CFR Part 20, the TS, and licensee’s procedural requirements.

preconditions.text: If direct observation is not possible at the time of inspection, review records, and discussions with responsible personnel for the most recent fuel movement activity.

constraints.text: Personnel dosimetry is required by 10 CFR 20.1502 and is to be used as specified by the licensee’s procedures.
The structural integrity of some reactors may be a required security barrier.
New fuel is required to be kept in the security area and locked in a fuel vault or in some way secured so that it cannot be removed.

preconditions.text: If direct observation is not possible at the time of the inspection, problem areas may be identified by the review of the licensee’s logs and records, and discussions with responsible personnel for the most recent fuel movement activity.

If direct observation is not possible at the time of inspection, the review of respective licensee logs and records, and discussions with responsible personnel for the most recent fuel movement activity will provide an acceptable sample for this inspection requirement.
